dominants.dominant_desc: To do this technique, you have to form an Arrow in the opening as the title says, first, go to the mid lane and help your mage clear the wave, that forms a line, then go to your jungler to help with the purple or orange buff(whatever he's taking) and that forms a diagonal line, next help him with the next buff, that forms the bottom line, and finally return to mid to complete the triangle, after that, help the exp laner or marksman, look which of them if struggling more and help them with their lane, and when the turtle spawns, you zone out the enemies while your jungler takes the first turtle, this, securing an early advantage against the enemy

hero.hero_overview: Chip is a Tank/Support Roamer that is said to be lazy, he can teleport using the connected portals around the map by using his fourth skill "Why Walk?" He can spawn a portal using his ultimate, his allies and enemies can teleport but the allies gain additional buffs such as a shield and speed boost, the portal can be destroyed and can disappear in a period of time, he can also heal using his passive, restoring a massive amount of health
hero.hero_strength: Chip excels in healing, so he doesn't need guardian helmet and can also set using his first skill and his ultimate, he can perfectly initiate a team fight even when he is alone because his portals can make his allies teleport to his location
hero.hero_weakness: Like most roamers, this hero's only weakness is bad allies and a bad user, he will initiate a team fight by himself and his teammates won't teleport, that's the big issue so it's better to invite some friends to play and good communication can be crucial due to his teleporting skills. Due to his reliance on the team, he is not a quite ideal choice for solo rank but it is one of the best choices for five man rank

spell.spell_desc: Once he hits the enemies with his second skill and casts his ultimate, his teammate might take a while to come so he uses flicker to exit out of combat and to restore lost hp (healing)

hero.hero_overview: Chip is a support/tank hero in Mobile Legends known for his unique teleportation and map control abilities.His kit focuses on using portals to reposition allies, initiate fights, and apply crowd control.
hero.hero_strength: Chip's main strengths are teleportation, high crowd control, and strong survivability, making him a powerful support/tank that can roam, surprise enemies, and protect allies
hero.hero_weakness: Chip's main weakness is being vulnerable to CC (crowd control) heroes like Tigreal, Barats, and Minsitthar, which prevent him from using his healing ability or escaping damage

spell.spell_desc: Chip doesn't have a built-in blink or dash, so Flicker is essential for repositioning, escaping, or initiating fights.

dominants.dominant_desc: First you have to help your jungle get a buff, second you will help your mage level up quickly to rotate to the gold lane, after that check the enemy jungle .

If possible, you can cause trouble in the opposing team's jungle by asking the team for backup. If you win the fight in the opponent's jungle area, you can continue rotating to the exp lane to help win their lane.
if you have reached lvl 3 you can ask the mage to leave some minions for you so you can reach lvl 4 first, and the team mage goes to the exp lane to get lvl 4
After you reach level 4, you can initiate a war in the turtle area by controlling some of the bushes because you already have the ultimate, don't waste it.
When you know the position of the enemy jungle, give information to the team where the enemy jungle is, and inform the team if you want to open a war when the enemy jungle appears.

If you want to initiate a war on the opponent's jungle, you really have to pay attention to your position so you don't make mistakes and you have to pay attention to whether there is a followback from the team.
when you have initiated prevent the enemy jungle from approaching the turtle. when the enemy jungle is defeated your team has won the first war.
you can do the same thing as when the first war turtle

I recommend this build for long term war

spell.spell_desc: Either you can choose petrify or flicker but I prefer flicker because you can use flicker and skill 2 and skill 3.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Auorora is a great mage for team fights .. with her AOE skills that has a lot of crowd control effect . She is one of my best teammate

hanabi synergizes well with chip too . Hanabi shows her maximum capability when multiple enemies are near with each other. With her attacks that bounces back on enemies , she is a great teammate when using chip

counters.counter_desc: Chips greatest enemy along the roams is carmilla .. one wrong move with your ultimate skill , then carmilla will just place her ultimate .. then all your gold  advantage will begone.  Carmilla's ultimate works best the more enemies are linked with each other  .

dominants.dominant_desc:
You have to get both you 1st and 2nd before you pick a fight , so you could use your combo ( if not you may die if you dont have  your first skill ) 

Gold and Exp is the easiest to gank because your portal is near them 

Utilize your beacons wisely because it has lot of cooldown (60 seconds ) 

In the early phase , just use 2nd and 1st to squishy enemies to poke them . ( So they will be annoyed or recall to their base )

Always try to catch isolated enemies using you 'arrival' or your 'beacons '

Carefully think if you have to use your 3rd skill ( shortcut / main portal ) in calling your allies .. Because they could die if you are in a disadvantage fight.. ( Just die by yourself if you think your team will not win )

Late game is sometimes the hardest part because enemies tend to group up .. so its hard to place you 3rd skill  or your Portal to them ..

 try to freeze waves   away from their base so they will try to farm creeps and extend away from their team.. 

Its also critical to try to protect your allies in lategame , like the marksman or the mage .. because that may be the only thing that you can do at those times

emblems.emblem_desc:
I always use the two mobility emblems along with the mobility boots , for easy roaming ( since his 4th skill is nerfed on 08-2024 ) 

Concussive blast along with dire hit  to poke enemies or sometimes kill squishy heroes.

equips.equip_desc:
Thunderbelt is my main equipment for chip because of its control ability  and ability to stack defense 

Dominance for enemies with high healing , high attack soeed , and also for Chips extra speed

Antique Cuirass for those enemies who like to assassinate my teammates , this could reduce their physical damage percentage

Athena for those high burs assassins magic damage.. sometimes i also buil twilight .

 And finally immortality for late game fights , so i could just dive in and die

hero.hero_overview: Chip is a initiator and sometimes guard hero that utilizes his portals to quickly aid his teammates or call them for a teamfight.
hero.hero_strength:
Anti asssassin or low health heroes : By using his 2nd skill then 1st skill , he could easily poke enemy heroes and also gain a stack for his thunderbelt

Anti splitpush : with his 4th skill ' why walk ' , he could use these beacons to easily visit the 4 corners of the map .. Additionally , the spell ' arrival ' can also be use in this cases

Teamfight Outnumbering: By properly observing where are the enemies, Chip can use his 3rd skill 'shortcut' to an isolated enemy for easy kill.
hero.hero_weakness:
Because his strength is calling his teammates using his 3rd skill "shortcut" .. Enemies with AOE skills hard counter him such as ( Tigreal , Atlas , Carmilla )

Also fighters who excels when they are already near the enemy , such as ( Freya , Alpha , Yuzhong , etc..)


( However due to his recent nerf 11-2024 , he cannot even fight assassins too )

spell.spell_desc:
Although the main spell that was use by many is Flicker, petrify and vengeance. 

I always use arrival , which can be used to : 

- quickly gank enemies, if you are far from your beacons 
- prevent enemies from split pushing
- sneakily teleport to Lord 
- protect base and turrets
- and sometimes to escape

cooperates.cooperate_desc:
Synergy: Faramis excels as a Support/Mage, providing healing and crowd control. His ultimate, Cult Altar, can revive allies, making him an excellent partner for Chip.

Reason: Chip can initiate fights and control enemies with his crowd control, while Faramis ensures that allies stay alive and can re-engage after being downed. Their combined abilities create a formidable frontline that can withstand heavy damage.

Synergy: Carmilla's ability to link enemies and deal AoE damage pairs well with Chip's crowd control. When Chip engages with his skills, Carmilla can follow up with her ultimate to maximize damage.

Reason: Together, they can effectively lock down enemies while dealing significant damage, making them a strong duo in team fights.

Synergy: Benedetta's mobility and burst damage complement Chip's tankiness and crowd control. Chip can absorb damage while Benedetta dives into the backline to eliminate key targets.

Reason: Their combination allows for aggressive plays, where Chip creates space for Benedetta to maneuver and deal damage without fear of immediate retaliation.

Synergy: X.Borg's durability and area damage complement Chip's tankiness and crowd control. Together, they can create a frontline that is hard to break through.
Reason: Chip can absorb damage while X.Borg deals AoE damage, making them a formidable duo in team fights.

Synergy with Odette
Crowd Control and Damage: Chip's crowd control abilities, particularly from Skill 1 (Crash Course) and Skill 3 (Shortcut), complement Odette's burst damage potential. When Chip initiates fights, Odette can follow up with her ultimate, Dancing Blades, to maximize damage on immobilized enemies.

Team Fight Coordination: Chip's ability to teleport allies using his portals allows Odette to reposition quickly during team fights, enabling her to deal damage safely.

Synergy: Gatot's durability and area damage complement Chip's crowd control. Chip can initiate fights while Gatot provides sustained damage and protection for allies.

Reason: Their combination allows for a strong frontline that can absorb damage while dealing significant pressure on enemies.

counters.counter_desc:
Counter Relationship: Hayabusa's mobility allows him to bypass Chip's defenses and target squishy allies directly. His ultimate can also eliminate Chip quickly if he is caught alone.

Reason: Hayabusa's ability to deal damage while being elusive makes it hard for Chip to provide effective support during engagements.

Counter Relationship: Benedetta's mobility allows her to evade Chip's crowd control and quickly eliminate him or his allies. She can also use her skills to reposition during fights, making it hard for Chip to land effective engagements.

Reason: If Benedetta targets Chip first, she can burst him down before he has a chance to use his abilities effectively

Counter Relationship: Franco's hook can catch Chip off guard, isolating him from his team and preventing him from using his abilities effectively.

Reason: If Franco lands his hook on Chip, he can quickly eliminate him before he has a chance to engage or escape.

Counter Relationship: Gatot's ultimate can counter Chip's crowd control by providing invulnerability to allies during its duration.

Reason: If Gatot uses his ultimate effectively, he can negate Chip's impact in team fights.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Roaming Strategy:
Utilize Chip's exceptional mobility with Skill 2 (Overtime) to roam effectively. Start by assisting your mid-lane mage and jungler to secure buffs and objectives. This helps establish map control early on.
dominants.dominant_title: Early Game Tips and Tricks for Chip, Before Level 4 (1-3)

dominants.dominant_desc:
Ganking Opportunities:
Look for opportunities to gank the gold lane before the first turtle spawns. Use Skill 1 (Crash Course) to initiate, dealing area damage and applying Chip's Mark to enemies, setting them up for follow-up attacks.
dominants.dominant_title: Early Game Tips and Tricks for Chip, Before Level 4 (2-3)

dominants.dominant_desc:
Stay Close to Allies:
Position yourself near allied laners during wave clear to gain extra experience. This will help you level up quickly, enhancing your skills and overall impact in the game.
dominants.dominant_title: Early Game Tips and Tricks for Chip, Before Level 4 (3-3)

dominants.dominant_desc:
Health Management:
Monitor your health closely. Use Snack Time! (passive ability) strategically to heal when low, allowing you to stay in lane longer without returning to base.

Map Awareness:
Keep an eye on enemy movements, especially potential invades on your jungler. Be ready to assist with vision and crowd control if enemies try to contest buffs.

Initiate with Crash Course:
In team fights, start by using Skill 1 (Crash Course) to hit multiple enemies, maximizing your shield and crowd control effect. This ability not only deals damage but also sets the stage for your team's follow-up attacks.

Utilize Overtime for Mobility:
Activate Skill 2 (Overtime) before engaging in fights to increase your movement speed and knock back enemies, creating space for your allies or disrupting enemy formations.

Strategic Portal Usage:
Position your ultimate, Shortcut, wisely during skirmishes. Use it to teleport allies into the fight or provide an escape route if things go south. This can turn the tide of battle by allowing for quick reinforcements or retreats.

Target Priority:
Focus on marking key enemy targets with your abilities, especially squishy heroes or those with high damage output. By doing so, you enhance your team's damage against them through Focusing Mark, ensuring that your team capitalizes on every engagement.

emblems.emblem_desc: This Support Emblem set enhances Chip's core strengths as a Tank/Roamer by improving his healing capabilities, mobility, and ability to contribute damage when needed. By choosing this emblem set, you maximize Chip's potential to influence team fights and provide crucial support to your allies throughout the game. This strategic choice allows you to excel in both roaming and team engagements, making you a formidable presence on the battlefield!
emblems.emblem_title: Chip's Support Emblem Mastery: Elevate Your Gameplay"

emblems.emblem_desc: The combination of "Pull Yourself Together" and "Focusing Mark" makes this Support Emblem set particularly effective for Chip. The former enhances his ability to use crucial spells more frequently, while the latter amplifies his team's damage output during engagements. Together, these talents allow Chip to excel in both sustaining his allies and enabling aggressive plays that can lead to victory.
emblems.emblem_title: "Unleashing Potential: Chip's Essential Support Emblem Guide"

equips.equip_desc: By selecting this equipment set, you are equipping Chip to fulfill his role as a tanky support hero effectively. The synergy between these items not only enhances his individual capabilities but also amplifies his impact on the team as a whole. With improved sustainability, cooldown management, crowd control, and magic resistance, Chip becomes an indispensable asset on the battlefield, empowering your team to secure victories in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang.

hero.hero_overview:
Background: Chip is the 124th hero in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang, classified as a Tank/Roamer. He utilizes portals for mobility and support.

Role: Chip excels in crowd control and team assistance, making him a valuable asset in skirmishes and objectives.

Farming Routes: He typically roams to assist mid-lane and jungle, securing buffs while preparing for ganks, especially before major objectives like Turtle or Lord
hero.hero_strength:
Laning Advantages: Strong mobility allows for quick rotations and ganks.

Team Fight Advantages: His ultimate can turn the tide by teleporting allies into battle.

Game Phases: Effective throughout all phases; excels in early ganks and late-game ambushes.
hero.hero_weakness:
Laning Disadvantages: Vulnerable to burst damage without proper positioning.

Team Fight Disadvantages: Long cooldown on his ultimate can leave him less effective if misused.

Game Phase Weaknesses: May struggle against high-damage heroes in the early game

data.recommend: I highly recommend this comprehensive guide for players looking to master Chip in MLBB. This guide provides in-depth insights into Chip's abilities, optimal builds, and effective strategies.

spell.spell_desc:
Sprint
Description: Sprint grants a significant boost to movement speed for a short duration, allowing Chip to quickly reposition himself on the battlefield or escape from dangerous situations. This spell is particularly useful for enhancing his mobility, enabling him to roam effectively and assist teammates in ganks or rretreats. 

Engagement Initiation: Activate Sprint before entering a team fight to reach the frontline faster and set up for your ultimate.

data.title: Chip's Portal Power: A Comprehensive Guide to Dominating with MLBB Newest Hero
